name: Cerveny Kapusta
recipeIngredient: 3 cups Rendered pork fat (trimmed from roast)
recipeInstructions:
1. Render pork fat very slowly; remove the cracklings and add enough finely chopped onion for taste and flavor. Cook until golden.
2. Add paprika slowly—don't let it burn or smoke. Let cool, then store, covered in the refrigerator (it will keep for a long time).
3. Wash the cabbage in cold water, quarter it, and chop finely, salting as you go. Discard the core.
4. Heat about 3 cups of the pork-fat/onion mixture; add about 1/2 teaspoon paprika—don't let it burn.
5. Add the wet, salty cabbage and stir until it's coated with fat.
6. Add pepper to taste, grated apple, then stir in.
7. Add about 2-3 tablespoons white vinegar, 1 tablespoon sugar. Rub about 1 1/2 teaspoons caraway seeds between palms and add to cabbage.
This recipe is handed down through the Wincenc family and will yield different results each time until you find your preferred taste. Taste as you go along! The true test comes on the second or third day when reheating leftovers.
